title: linum narbonense subsp. narbonense
intro: Linum narbonense subsp. narbonense, also known as Fat Hen or Wild Mallow, is a subspecies of the Linum narbonense plant, a flowering plant in the mallow family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and eye problems. The health benefits of Linum narbonense subsp. narbonense include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and soothe irritated skin, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or topical salve to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
